items.q: Why does 'Me gusta el chocolate' literally translate as 'Chocolate is pleasing to me'?
items.correct: Gustar works backwards - the thing liked is the subject, the person is the indirect object.
items.misconception: Gustar means 'to like' and works exactly like English.
items.random_wrong: It is a typo for 'Yo gusto'.
items.why: Students translate gustar as 'to like' with English structure. Actually gustar = 'to be pleasing to'. So 'me gustan los libros' (books are pleasing to me) - the verb agrees with the thing, not the liker.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: Which verb is used to say 'I am happy today' (temporary state)?
items.why: Students treat ser and estar as identical. SER for permanent traits, identity, time, origin. ESTAR for temporary states, location, ongoing actions. 'Soy contento' would suggest 'I am a happy person', not 'I'm happy right now'.

items.q: What is the surname of the family in Cien años de soledad?
items.why: Correct answer: Buendía. “Trueba” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: When did García Márquez win the Nobel Prize?
items.why: Correct answer: 1982. “1972” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was Cien años de soledad published?
items.why: The real figure is 1967. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
